question: Are there exactly three king crabs depicted in the image?
Do the subjects clearly retain the broad, spiky carapace and segmented leg structure of king crabs, avoiding humanoid body shapes?
Is the central crab positioned upright with its large claws raised high in a Y-shape, matching the center figure in the pose diagram?
Are the two side crabs positioned low to the ground in a kneeling or worshipping pose, facing inward toward the central crab?
Are the spines on the crab shells transformed into hybrid ornaments that combine mechanical gears with Rococo scrollwork?
Are the pincers modified into mechanical arms that incorporate visible steam pipes?
Does the image predominantly feature a color palette of pink-gold and antique bronze tones?
Does the aesthetic successfully blend Rococo elements (delicate scrollwork, elegance) with Steampunk elements (machinery, gears)?
Are the mechanical modifications blended naturally with the biological crab anatomy, avoiding a 'pasted-on' look?
